title: Import .xlsx project plan and achieve hierarchy view, eg, parent and subtask
excerpt: Hi Monday.com community … trying to import a .xlsx from a .mpp (MS Project file) with a hierarchy. Can get the outline levels incorporated as a plan but cannot model the parent/sub task view without a TON of manual input…

posts.author: GRH (Graham Holford)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi Monday.com community … trying to import a .xlsx from a .mpp (MS Project file) with a hierarchy. Can get the outline levels incorporated as a plan but cannot model the parent/sub task view without a TON of manual input … has anyone out there got a solution for this? Ojala! thanks

posts.author: jtothejz (Jake Zimmer)
posts.cookedHtml:
Did you ever find a solution here? We’re moving from Asana and from the integration I assumed subtasks would get dropped in cleanly since Monday supports them (and Asana’s export provides them)

This seems like a no brainer for any company moving into Monday that they will want import functionality on the subtasks.

posts.author: AngelaVelasco (Angela Velasco)
posts.cookedHtml: I agree, I had to spend laborious hours manually defining 1,000 tasks.

posts.author: FlipperMan (Chris)
posts.cookedHtml: How can the marketing impress on potential users the benefits of moving from MS Project to Monday.com and then NOT offer a simple import for existing MS Project plans ? Bizarre.

posts.author: jenny.yang (Jenny)
posts.cookedHtml: Was any solution provided? Welcome on hearing what others have / are doing (and not the manual method!).
